root_causes_found: New ad photos were stored as Base64 strings directly in ads.image_url and ads.images. Public list and detail requests could therefore transfer megabytes of JSON before the page rendered.
The My Ads API selected every column from up to 500 ads, including all Base64 images.
Every route downloaded and executed Post Ad, OTP, filters and authentication helper scripts, even when those features were not used.
Full-document MutationObservers and 900 ms polling loops stayed active after page mount.
Ad detail routes depended on the full React marketplace bundle and could remain blank when a data relationship query failed.
fixes: Ad detail pages now use a dedicated lightweight page and direct metadata API.
Public metadata APIs exclude image columns and use a lazy binary image proxy.
New uploads are stored in Supabase Storage and only public URLs are saved in the ads table.
Old Base64 ads continue to display through /api/ad-image, and an optional migration script is included.
My Ads queries only the signed-in user and excludes raw image columns.
Route-specific script loading prevents unrelated helper bundles from running.
Permanent observers and polling intervals were removed or limited to the initial mount window.
